title: pelargonium × rubescens
intro: Pelargonium × rubescens, also known as the copper rose or zambesiense, is a species of flowering plant in the geranium family, characterized by its bright pink flowers and reddish stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Pelargonium × rubescens include relief from colds and flu, reduction of fever, and soothing of sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
